documents.text: The [BRAND] [MODEL] fixed-base wood router is built for clean edge work, dado cuts, and laminate trimming. Its 1.75 HP motor delivers 10,000 to 25,000 RPM with electronic feedback to maintain speed under load. A fine-depth ring allows precise bit adjustment in 1/64-inch increments, while the die-cast aluminum base improves stability on hardwood and plywood. This router accepts 1/4-inch and 1/2-inch shank bits and includes a clear sub-base for better line visibility. Soft-start control, spindle lock, and a dust adapter make it practical for both shop and site use.

Designed for cabinetmaking and template work, the [BRAND] [MODEL] plunge wood router combines power with accurate control. The 2.25 HP motor offers variable speed from 8,000 to 22,000 RPM, making it suitable for large profile bits and slower hardwood cuts. Smooth plunge travel with a three-stop turret supports repeatable mortises, grooves, and hinge recesses. Ergonomic handles, a trigger lock, and above-table height adjustment increase convenience during longer tasks. The router includes both 1/4-inch and 1/2-inch collets, a chip shield, and an onboard fine-tune mechanism for consistent depth settings.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] compact trim router is a lightweight option for edge profiling, flush trimming, and decorative detail work. Powered by a 6.5-amp motor, it reaches 30,000 RPM for fast, clean cuts in softwood, MDF, and laminates. Its slim body improves one-handed control, while the quick-release motor clamp makes base changes simple. An adjustable clear base helps maintain visibility around the bit, and the spindle lock speeds up accessory swaps. The [BRAND] [MODEL] accepts 1/4-inch shank bits and includes a straight guide, dust nozzle, and rubber overmold grip.

For woodworkers who want flexibility, the [BRAND] [MODEL] router combo kit includes both fixed and plunge bases with one 2 HP motor. Variable speed ranges from 9,000 to 24,000 RPM, allowing better performance with small trimming bits or larger panel-raising cutters. The fixed base features micro-adjust depth control and a low center of gravity for smooth edge routing. The plunge base provides 2 inches of travel, cushioned handles, and a turret stop for repeated cuts. LED work lights, soft start, and interchangeable 1/4-inch and 1/2-inch collets round out this versatile package.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] heavy-duty wood router is intended for demanding joinery and raised-panel applications. A 15-amp, 3.25 HP motor supplies strong torque and a variable speed range of 10,000 to 21,000 RPM. Constant-speed circuitry helps maintain cut quality through dense oak, maple, and hardwood composites. The oversized handles provide better control, and the precision depth adjustment allows repeatable settings when making grooves or dovetail jig passes. This model accepts 1/2-inch and 1/4-inch bits, includes a wrench set and edge guide, and is compatible with many router tables for dedicated shop use.

Ideal for finish carpentry and light shop tasks, the [BRAND] [MODEL] palm router delivers dependable trimming performance in a compact body. Its 5.8-amp motor spins from 12,000 to 28,000 RPM, giving users flexibility for laminate, veneer, and round-over work. The square fixed base improves balance on narrow edges, while the transparent base plate helps track the cut line. A rack-and-pinion depth system provides simple, accurate adjustment without tools. The [BRAND] [MODEL] uses 1/4-inch bits only and includes a guide fence, template bushing adapter, and dust extraction port for cleaner operation.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] plunge router is designed for precise inlays, mortises, and pattern routing. A 12-amp motor with soft start delivers 8,000 to 23,000 RPM, and the electronic control system reduces speed drop during heavier passes. Dual guide posts offer smooth, stable plunging, and the adjustable depth rod pairs with a multi-stop turret for repeatable setups. Comfortable side handles and a low-vibration housing help reduce fatigue in extended use. This router supports 1/4-inch and 1/2-inch collets, includes a chip deflector and edge guide, and features a sealed switch for improved dust resistance.

Built for hobbyists and serious DIY users, the [BRAND] [MODEL] fixed-base router offers straightforward control and reliable cutting power. Its 11-amp motor produces up to 27,000 RPM, making it suitable for chamfers, rabbets, dados, and decorative edging. The aluminum motor housing adds durability without excessive weight, and the coarse plus fine depth adjustment system helps dial in exact bit projection. A flat-top design lets the router rest upside down during bit changes, and the spindle lock simplifies maintenance. The [BRAND] [MODEL] comes with a 1/4-inch collet, parallel guide, and removable dust hood.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] cordless trim router provides mobility for punch-list jobs, onsite fitting, and quick edge detail work. Running on an 18V battery platform, it delivers a no-load speed range of 10,000 to 26,000 RPM with electronic speed selection for different materials. Brushless motor technology improves runtime and reduces maintenance, while the compact body supports easy one-hand operation. An LED light brightens the cutting area, and the depth ring offers fine adjustment for laminate and softwood tasks. The router accepts 1/4-inch bits and includes a straight base, edge guide, and vacuum adapter.

Made for router table installations, the [BRAND] [MODEL] production router emphasizes power and above-table convenience. Its 3 HP motor runs from 10,000 to 22,000 RPM, with speed control optimized for larger diameter bits used in cabinet doors, cope-and-stick joints, and molding work. An above-table height crank allows easy adjustment without removing the motor, and the sealed bearing design helps withstand fine dust in busy shops. The [BRAND] [MODEL] supports 1/2-inch and 1/4-inch collets, features soft start and overload protection, and includes a motor housing designed for stable table mounting.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] mid-size wood router balances power and handling for general woodworking projects. A 1.5 HP motor with variable speed from 10,000 to 25,000 RPM works well for edge shaping, hinge mortising, and template trimming. Its clear round sub-base improves visibility, while the ergonomic handles give better control on narrow stock. Tool-free motor removal makes bit changes and base cleaning faster. A fine adjustment dial helps set exact depth for repeat passes, and the router accepts both 1/4-inch and 1/2-inch bits. The package includes an edge guide, wrench, and dust collection attachment.

For detailed furniture work, the [BRAND] [MODEL] precision trim router focuses on accuracy and comfort. The 7-amp motor offers variable speed between 12,000 and 30,000 RPM, allowing clean cuts on hardwood edges, plywood veneer, and plastics. A micro-depth adjustment system supports delicate flush trimming and small-profile routing, while the low-profile base keeps the tool stable near corners. Rubberized grip areas improve control during one-handed use, and the transparent base keeps the bit visible. The [BRAND] [MODEL] takes 1/4-inch shank bits and includes an offset base, guide roller, and dust nozzle.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] professional plunge router is suited to deep cuts, joinery, and workshop jig use. Its 2.5 HP motor produces 9,000 to 21,000 RPM with constant-speed electronics for smoother results in dense material. The plunge mechanism offers 2-3/8 inches of travel and locks firmly in position once depth is set. A rotating turret stop speeds up repeated passes, especially for mortises and stopped grooves. The [BRAND] [MODEL] includes 1/2-inch and 1/4-inch collets, oversized handles, a built-in chip shield, and an edge fence for improved guidance along boards and panels.

Compact but capable, the [BRAND] [MODEL] woodworking router is designed for homeowners upgrading from basic tools. The 9.5-amp motor reaches 11,000 to 26,000 RPM, making it versatile enough for shelf dados, corner round-overs, and small decorative profiles. A simple cam-lock system lets users adjust the motor depth quickly, while the cast base plate keeps the router steady on flat stock. The switch is sealed against dust, and the cord exits high on the housing to stay clear of the work surface. Included are a 1/4-inch collet, guide fence, and template guide adapter.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] dual-handle fixed-base router offers steady control for long edge passes and pattern routing. A 2 HP, 12-amp motor provides 10,000 to 24,000 RPM, with soft-start behavior that reduces initial twist when the tool powers on. Precision depth settings are managed through a coarse release and fine-thread adjuster, useful when matching profiles or setting exact groove depth. The wide base opening accepts larger guide bushings, and the balanced housing reduces chatter. This [BRAND] [MODEL] includes both 1/4-inch and 1/2-inch collets, a straight edge guide, and a vacuum-compatible dust port.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] compact plunge router is a versatile choice for users who need controlled entry cuts in a smaller format. Its 8.5-amp motor runs from 10,000 to 25,000 RPM and is suitable for inlay recesses, sign making, and trim carpentry. Spring-loaded plunge action feels smooth and predictable, while the depth rod and turret stop allow repeatable cuts without overcomplicating setup. A clear chip guard improves visibility near the bit, and the soft-grip handles increase comfort. The router uses 1/4-inch bits, includes a circle guide, and connects to standard dust extraction hoses.
